exercises.prompt: « Ce n'est pas faux. » — which device, and what does it mean?

options.text: Litote — it means « vous avez raison »
Allusion — it quotes a famous novel

exercises.explanation: Litotes says less to mean more: not-wrong = right.

exercises.prompt: Your friend surveys a disastrous kitchen: « Bravo, vraiment, quelle organisation ! » Device?

options.text: Antiphrase — the praise is the attack

exercises.explanation: Antiphrasis states the opposite of what's meant; the tone does the work.

exercises.prompt: The exam move: name the device, translate it.
exercises.template: Par {{b1}}, l'auteur suggère en réalité le contraire de ce qu'il écrit.
exercises.explanation: Antiphrase = the opposite-saying device. Name it, then give the real meaning.

exercises.template: Partie de rien, elle a gravi tous les {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Gravir les échelons — the fixed idiom of social climbing.

exercises.prompt: « Trader, un métier d'avenir ? » — what is the question mark doing?

options.text: Carrying the irony: the title quietly doubts what it states
Asking readers to send in answers

exercises.explanation: An ironic title states and doubts at once; punctuation is part of the argument.

exercises.prompt: Where the real meaning lives (three words: entre les …).
exercises.template: À ce niveau, il faut lire entre les {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Lire entre les lignes = to read between the lines.

exercises.prompt: Match each device to its definition.
Put the words in order: "She climbed every rung."
Listen to the tone. What does the speaker really mean?
exercises.transcript: Il n'est pas sot, votre nouveau collègue — ce n'est pas tous les jours qu'on gravit trois échelons en un an.

options.text: High praise by litote: the colleague is sharp and rising fast
An insult: the colleague is foolish

exercises.explanation: « Il n'est pas sot » (litote = he's sharp) + the rare three-rung climb = admiration, understated.

exercises.prompt: Read this column opening and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: DES VERTUS DE LA PARESSE — Réjouissons-nous : nous avons enfin inventé le salarié parfait, joignable à minuit, fier de déjeuner devant son écran. Quel progrès ! Nos grands-parents, qui ont lutté pour les congés payés, seraient sans doute émus de voir l'usage que nous en faisons. La paresse, elle, attendra : il paraît que ce n'est pas rentable.
exercises.passageEn: ON THE VIRTUES OF LAZINESS — Let us rejoice: we have finally invented the perfect employee, reachable at midnight, proud to lunch in front of a screen. What progress! Our grandparents, who fought for paid holidays, would no doubt be moved to see the use we make of them. As for laziness, it can wait: apparently it isn't profitable.

questions.prompt: « Réjouissons-nous… Quel progrès ! » — what is the author really doing?

options.text: Denouncing overwork by antiphrase — the celebration is the critique
Asking readers to work at midnight

questions.prompt: The mention of grandparents and paid holidays is…

options.text: an allusion to the social struggles (congés payés, 1936) that frames the irony

exercises.explanation: Travailler au noir = to work undeclared — no contract, no protection.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: « Ce n'est pas faux » : comprenez « vous avez raison ».

options.text: "Bravo, what organisation!" — the antiphrasis means the opposite.
Through antiphrasis, the author actually denounces the cult of productivity.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Une allusion à Victor Hugo. » — an allusion.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: « Ce n'est pas faux » : comprenez « vous avez raison ».

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Par antiphrase, l'auteur dénonce en réalité le culte de la productivité.
exercises.targetEn: Through antiphrasis, the author actually denounces the cult of productivity.
